quote: The details are not the details. They make the design.
through_line: CSS is philosophy in miniature today: when you build features that do more with less, you discover something about what design actually is. Data follows. Then: the nonself, the question of whether intelligence has a fixed shape, and the gentle dissolution of both.

articles.title: CSS in 2026: The new features reshaping frontend development
articles.pitch: New CSS features in 2026 — customizable selects, sibling-index(), scroll-state queries — are replacing patterns that once required hundreds of lines of JavaScript. A well-illustrated overview of where the platform is headed and how to start using these features thoughtfully.

articles.title: Data-Driven Decision-Making: The Key to DMO Marketing Success
articles.pitch: First-party data is not just a cookie-era substitute — it’s the core asset of DMO marketing in a privacy-first world. Sojern’s piece makes the case clearly: DMOs that build data collection and activation capacity now will be far better positioned in an increasingly cookieless landscape.

articles.title: Getting in Touch with Our Nonself Elements
articles.pitch: Interbeing and non-self aren’t abstract Buddhist doctrine — they’re direct descriptions of what you are, if you look carefully. This Still Water essay offers a contemplative practice: examining which ‘elements’ — cosmic, geological, cultural, ancestral — make up what you call yourself.

articles.title: Intelligence Evolved at Least Twice in Vertebrate Animals
articles.pitch: Birds like crows and ravens solve problems as complex as chimpanzees do, but their brains look completely different. New research confirms the surprising answer: intelligence independently evolved twice in vertebrates, arriving at similar circuits from different starting points. A beautiful piece on convergent cognition.
